subject: Human Resources
title: The Hawthorne Studies
description: A fast breakdown of The Hawthorne Studies for builders.

hook.text: In the 1920s, researchers at a Chicago factory changed the lighting. Productivity went up. They dimmed it. It went up again.

buildup.text: The Hawthorne Studies at Western Electric tried to find optimal working conditions through experiments.

discovery.text: The surprise finding: workers performed better simply because someone was paying attention to them.

twist.text: It wasn't the lighting that mattered—it was the feeling of being valued. This became the 'Hawthorne Effect.'

climax.text: These studies launched the human relations movement—proving that psychology matters as much as wages.

punchline.text: People work harder when they know someone cares.

quiz.question: What did the Hawthorne Studies discover?
options: Attention and feeling valued improved worker performance
Higher wages were the only motivator
